method: Claims are classified as documented fact, reasonable inference, or not established. Primary OpenAI sources are preferred.

claims.claim: Codex, ChatGPT Work, ChatGPT for Excel and Workspace Agents draw from the same agentic usage and credit pool when available.
Work usage inside ChatGPT uses the same pricing, credits and usage limits as Codex.
After included limits are reached, credits can be used across supported features including Codex and ChatGPT Work.
If an eligible user enables Auto top-up, the shared credit balance can trigger a purchase when it falls below the configured minimum.
Credits are generally non-refundable and expire 12 months after purchase, subject to applicable law.
Image generation uses the same general limits and consumes included usage around three to five times faster on average, depending on quality and size.
For a user who uses both products, Work activity can reduce the included capacity remaining for Codex because both draw from one allowance.
No official source located as of the verification date establishes that an ordinary Chat conversation automatically becomes Work usage merely because it calls an MCP tool.
This project does not claim evidence that OpenAI intentionally designed the disclosure to deceive consumers.
